2604.12617.title: SOAR: Self-Correction for Optimal Alignment and Refinement in Diffusion Models
2604.12617.paper_summary: The post-training pipeline for diffusion models currently has two stages: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on curated data and reinforcement learning (RL) with reward models. A fundamental gap separates them. SFT optimizes the denoiser only on ground-truth states sampled from the forward noising process; once inference deviates from these ideal states, subsequent denoising relies on out-of-distribution generalization rather than learned correction, exhibiting the same exposure bias that afflicts autoregressive models, but accumulated along the denoising trajectory instead of the token sequence. RL can in principle address this mismatch, yet its terminal reward signal is sparse, suffers from credit-assignment difficulty, and risks reward hacking. We propose SOAR (Self-Correction for Optimal Alignment and Refinement), a bias-correction post-training method that fills this gap. Starting from a real sample, SOAR performs a single stop-gradient rollout with the current model, re-noises the resulting off-trajectory state, and supervises the model to steer back toward the original clean target. The method is on-policy, reward-free, and provides dense per-timestep supervision with no credit-assignment problem. On SD3.5-Medium, SOAR improves GenEval from 0.70 to 0.78 and OCR from 0.64 to 0.67 over SFT, while simultaneously raising all model-based preference scores. In controlled reward-specific experiments, SOAR surpasses Flow-GRPO in final metric value on both aesthetic and text-image alignment tasks, despite having no access to a reward model. Since SOAR's base loss subsumes the standard SFT objective, it can directly replace SFT as a stronger first post-training stage after pretraining, while remaining fully compatible with subsequent RL alignment.
2604.12617.paper_summary_zh: 擴散模型的後訓練流程目前有兩個階段：在經過精選的數據上進行的監督微調（SFT）和使用獎勵模型的強化學習（RL）。它們之間存在根本的差距。SFT 僅在從前向噪聲過程中抽樣的真實狀態上優化去噪器；一旦推斷偏離這些理想狀態，隨後的去噪依賴於分佈外的泛化，而不是學習的修正，顯示出與自回歸模型相同的曝光偏差，但是在去噪軌跡上累積，而不是在標記序列上。RL 原則上可以解決這種不匹配，但其終端獎勵信號稀疏，面臨信用分配困難，並且存在獎勵黑客的風險。我們提出了 SOAR（自我修正以實現最佳對齊和精煉），這是一種填補此差距的偏差修正後訓練方法。SOAR 從一個真實樣本開始，使用當前模型進行單次停止梯度的展開，重新對生成的偏離軌跡的狀態進行加噪，並監督模型使其重新朝向原始的乾淨目標。該方法是在線的，無獎勵的，並且提供每個時間步的密集監督，沒有信用分配問題。在 SD3.5-Medium 上，SOAR 將 GenEval 從 0.70 提高到 0.78，將 OCR 從 0.64 提高到 0.67，相較於 SFT，同時提高了所有基於模型的偏好分數。在控制獎勵特定的實驗中，SOAR 在美學和文本-圖像對齊任務的最終指標值上超越了 Flow-GRPO，儘管沒有訪問獎勵模型。由於 SOAR 的基本損失包含了標準 SFT 目標，因此它可以直接替代 SFT 作為預訓練後更強的第一個後訓練階段，同時與後續的 RL 對齊完全兼容。
2604.12617.author: You Qin et.al.
2604.12617.authors: You Qin, Linqing Wang, Hao Fei, Roger Zimmermann, Liefeng Bo, Qinglin Lu, Chunyu Wang
